mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by > 2% over 60 days in 70% of triggered periods, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with 20-day SPX returns during expansion phases, or the mechanism reverses direction when real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and decelerating nonfarm payrolls creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and force monetary tightening, producing a consistent negative relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes show a positive correlation with 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains above 0.04 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate reflects improving labor market tightness and rising wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during economic recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where UNRATE declines, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4.0%.
